description_long: This object is a rectangular block constructed from irregularly shaped, dark gray stones tightly fitted together. The stones vary in size and texture, with rough, jagged surfaces and visible seams between each rock, giving the block a rugged, natural appearance. The top and sides are uneven, with some stones protruding slightly, while the overall form remains rectilinear. The coloration is predominantly dark gray with subtle variations, suggesting weathered stone. There are no visible engravings, markings, or artificial features, and the block appears solid and heavy. This type of structure is commonly used as a barrier or retaining wall in urban parks, gardens, or along sidewalks.
description: A rugged, rectangular block made of tightly fitted, dark gray stones with rough, uneven surfaces. The block has a natural, weathered appearance and is solid and heavy.
description_view_0: Narrow end view showing the irregular, jagged arrangement of stones along the short side.
description_view_1: Long side view displaying the full length of the block and the interlocking pattern of large, flat stones.
description_view_2: Opposite narrow end view, similar to view 0, highlighting the rough, uneven stone faces.
description_view_3: Opposite long side view, showing a continuous, rugged stone surface with visible seams and texture.
attribute_location_context: urban park retaining wall or garden landscaping barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: The stones show minor surface irregularities and color variations, suggesting some natural weathering but no significant erosion or moss growth.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
